records.ety: [OE. garnischen, garnissen, OF. garnir to provide, strengthen, prepare, garnish, warn, F. garnir to provide, furnish, garnish, -- of German origin; cf. OHG. warnōn to provide, equip; akin to G. wahren to watch, E. aware, ware, wary, and cf. also E. warn. See Wary, -ish, and cf. Garment, Garrison.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Garnished (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Garnishing.]

defs: To decorate with ornamental appendages; to set off; to adorn; to embellish.
quotes: All within with flowers was garnished.

defs: To ornament, as a dish, with something laid about it; as, a dish garnished with parsley.
To warn by garnishment; to give notice to; to garnishee. See Garnishee, v. t.
Something added for embellishment; decoration; ornament; also, dress; garments, especially such as are showy or decorated.
quotes: So are you, sweet, Even in the lovely garnish of a boy.
Matter and figure they produce; For garnish this, and that for use.

defs: Something set round or upon a dish as an embellishment, such as parsley. See Garnish, v. t., 2.
A fee; specifically, in English jails, formerly an unauthorized fee demanded by the old prisoners of a newcomer.
